name: no-place-to-hide
description: Glenn Greenwald's insider account of the Edward Snowden revelations — the story of how Snowden exposed the NSA's massive surveillance programs, the legal and political battles that followed, and what it means for privacy in the digital age.
useCase: The definitive story of the Snowden revelations — the surveillance state, the whistleblower, and the fight for privacy in the 21st century.
knownRisks: Greenwald is a controversial figure; some critics argue he was too sympathetic to Snowden.
The book reflects 2014-era knowledge; some specific NSA programs have since changed.
The debate about surveillance vs. security remains politically polarized.
